preamble: The shift from the alveolar nasal [n] to the voiced alveolar stop [d] is a process known as denasalization. It occurs when the velum is raised, blocking nasal airflow while maintaining the same place of articulation. This is often part of a 'strengthening' process in specific prosodic positions.

examples.note: Word-initial nasals in Korean are often denasalized, sounding like voiced stops to non-native ears.
A systematic historical shift where all primary nasals became voiced stops roughly 100 years ago.
